idPolicy.stability: An emitted ID is stable. Reuse is forbidden, and retirement leaves a tombstone rather than reallocating the number.
idPolicy.wording: Summary and detail wording may improve when the failure family and remediation contract do not change; consumers match IDs and structured fields, never full prose.
idPolicy.retirement: A retired diagnostic remains registered with its last meaning and a replacement ID when one exists.
idPolicy.allocation: Allocate the lowest suitable unused ID in the owning range and add the typed Haxe ID, catalog entry, tests, and documentation in the same change.

severityPolicy.escalation: A call site may select only an allowed severity. Policy-driven escalation must preserve the ID; HXC2001 is informational by default and may become a warning under runtime diagnostics.

sourcePolicy.requirement: Every emitted compiler diagnostic has a primary Haxe source span. When no user construct exists, use the compilation or configuration anchor rather than a host-only path.
sourcePolicy.pathForm: Normalized repository/project-relative UTF-8 with forward slashes; JSON output must not expose an absolute checkout path.
sourcePolicy.coordinates: One-based lines and columns with an exclusive end point.

profilePolicy.unresolved: Use unresolved only when configuration failed before a valid source-semantics profile could be selected.

ranges.category: language lowering and unsupported source semantics
C ABI, export, layout, and ownership boundaries
interop, unsafe boundaries, bindgen, and platform capabilities
target API, metadata, macros, and DSL contracts
determinism, incremental state, and quality gates

diagnostics.name: framework-unavailable
diagnostics.summary: The Reflaxe framework could not be resolved during compiler bootstrap.
diagnostics.remediation: Use the scoped library HXML, pin or vendor Reflaxe, or install the compatible library revision.

diagnostics.name: invalid-configuration
diagnostics.summary: A target identity, carrier capability, profile, runtime, environment, standard, or related build option is invalid or contradictory.
diagnostics.remediation: Correct the named option or carrier mismatch; use `hxc inspect build` once M8 implements configuration inspection.

diagnostics.name: lowering-not-implemented
diagnostics.summary: The scaffold reached a language-lowering path that is intentionally not implemented.
diagnostics.remediation: Implement and test the corresponding Beads task; scaffold output is never executable program output.

diagnostics.name: unsupported-expression
diagnostics.summary: A typed Haxe expression has no supported C lowering.
diagnostics.remediation: Use a supported construct or implement its typed IR and C AST lowering with semantic tests.

diagnostics.name: runtime-policy-violation
diagnostics.summary: Source semantics require a runtime feature forbidden by the selected runtime policy.
diagnostics.remediation: Remove the source requirement, select an allowed runtime policy, or provide an explicit metal implementation.

diagnostics.name: runtime-feature-selected
diagnostics.summary: A root source semantic requirement selected an hxrt feature after direct C and local specialization were ineligible.
diagnostics.remediation: Inspect hxc.runtime-plan.json; accept the explained feature, choose a semantics-preserving typed C alternative, or select a stricter runtime policy.

diagnostics.name: invalid-abi-boundary
diagnostics.summary: An exported or imported declaration cannot be represented by the selected stable C ABI contract.
diagnostics.remediation: Use fixed ABI types, opaque handles, views, callbacks with context, and explicit status or error outputs.

diagnostics.name: raw-c-forbidden-in-example
diagnostics.summary: An example or snapshot uses raw `__c__()` target-code injection.
diagnostics.remediation: Add a typed `c.*` facade or compiler lowering instead.

diagnostics.name: raw-c-forbidden-in-strict-mode
diagnostics.summary: Strict mode found application-side raw `__c__()` target-code injection.
diagnostics.remediation: Use typed interop, a reviewed framework macro, or disable strict mode only for an isolated migration fixture.

diagnostics.name: bootstrap-macro-contract
diagnostics.summary: A public target macro is present as an API contract but its validated implementation is not complete.
diagnostics.remediation: Complete the owning Beads milestone before using the macro in production code.

diagnostics.name: invalid-typed-c-contract
diagnostics.summary: A typed C declaration, header, layout, ownership, build, macro, or DSL contract is invalid for the resolved target configuration.
diagnostics.remediation: Correct the reported typed declaration or metadata at its Haxe source span; do not bypass validation with raw C.

diagnostics.name: internal-compiler-error
diagnostics.summary: A required compiler lifecycle invariant or internal object is missing or malformed.
diagnostics.remediation: Report a minimized reproducer, compiler/version manifest, and the relevant stack trace or IR dump; do not bypass validation with raw C.
